title: Scene Blueprint v1
description: Declarative blueprint for a Daslab scene tree — typed slots, recurring agent tasks, evaluable scenarios, access grants. The blueprint is itself a scene-block: flat identity + content fields, with recursive `scenes[]` for children. v1 applier implements scene/assets/linkedAssets/schedules/members; other fields (inputs, secrets, extends, tasks, hooks, outputs) are reserved.

id.description: DB scene id, stable across applies. Either prefixed (scn_*) or a UUID for legacy roots.

parentId.description: Parent scene id. When this block is nested under another scene's `scenes[]`, the applier sets parentId from context; explicit value overrides.

settings.description: User-editable scene settings (matches the iOS scene-settings sheet). Description is here, not at the top, because it's a configurable label not an identity field.

description.description: Subtitle / blurb shown in scene settings.

welcomeSuggestions.description: Starter prompts shown above the input in iOS. Takes precedence over auto-defaults.

notifyOnComplete.description: Push notifications on job completion. Defaults to true.

inputs.description: Parameterization for blueprint reuse. ${inputs.X} references are substituted across the document at apply time. CLI binds via --input X=Y; unbound + no default + required:true throws.

secrets.description: (v1 stub — reserved) Secret references resolved via vault/env.

extends.description: (v1 stub — reserved) Base blueprint(s) this one extends.

layers.description: Logical partitions of scene state with independent commit chains. Assets reference these by `slug` via their `layer` field. If omitted, an implicit `default` layer holds all assets (v1.0 behavior preserved).

roles.description: Custom roles with per-layer read/write permissions. Built-in roles (owner|editor|viewer) remain available and cannot be shadowed.

public.description: When true, anonymous viewers assume this role. At most one role per scene can be public. Should declare only `read[]` (no `write[]`).

id.description: Plan-local handle; doubles as default externalId. Applier derives DB id via assetId(sceneId, type, externalId).

layer.description: Layer slug. Must reference a declared layer in `layers[]` (or the implicit `default`). Defaults to `default`.

externalId.description: Provider's native id. Stored on assets.external_id.

assetOwnerId.description: DB id of the parent account asset (reference to a row outside the blueprint).

pinned.description: (v1 stub — reserved) Field paths the plan always owns; user edits get discarded on re-apply.

linkedAssets.description: Pin existing rows to this scene. Three ref shapes: (a) direct DB id; (b) (type, externalId) resolved via assetId(rootSceneId, type, externalId); (c) (slot, accepts) — user-picked at apply time, materializes as a daslab/placeholder if not bound. Optional name/fields/externalUrl edit-in-place when a row resolves.

slot.description: Plan-local slot name. Forks: user picks a workspace asset of `accepts` type; placeholder otherwise.

accepts.description: Asset type the slot accepts (e.g. 'github/account', 'salesforce/contact'). Applier resolves to a workspace row of this type, or materializes a daslab/placeholder.

layer.description: Layer slug for this scene's link. Defaults to `default`. Does not affect the underlying assets row.

name.description: When set + resolved, UPDATE the existing row's name.

externalUrl.description: When set + resolved, UPDATE the existing row's external_url.

fields.description: When set + resolved, UPDATE the existing row's fields.

title.description: Optional display name. Defaults to `id` titlecased.

cron.description: Standard 5-field cron expression with optional `TZ=<IANA>` prefix. Examples: `0 6 * * *` (UTC daily 06:00), `TZ=Europe/Berlin 0 6 * * *` (Berlin local time, DST-aware). The cron string is the recurrence rule — `next_run_at` is derived on the DB row, never duplicated here.

prompt.description: The user-input prompt that fires on each tick. Lives directly on the schedule template — no separate phantom prompt_user call. The scheduler reads this at fire time and seeds a real prompt_user call on the freshly-minted child run.

anchors.description: Optional explicit input attachments. Each entry is an `AnchorRef` (scenecast canonical shape: `{ asset_id, anchor? }`). When omitted/empty, the scheduled run inherits the scene's default context (all visible assets). When present, behaves like a user pre-selecting these elements in the iOS composer before tapping Run — identical wire format. `asset_id` may be a blueprint-local handle (resolved by the applier) or a real db id.

anchor.description: Optional sub-element selector in scenecast anchor grammar: `item[<id>]`, `row[<idx>|<id>]`, `field[<key>]`, `step[<id>]`, `metric[<id>]`, `zone[<name>]`, `object[<id>]`, `surface[<id>]`, `point[<x>,<y>(,<z>)]`. Omit to address the whole asset.

runAs.description: Role slug to scope the run's layer permissions. References a `roles[]` slug or built-in (owner|editor|viewer). Defaults to the scene's owner-attribution.

tools.description: Optional override of the scene's tool allow/deny. Omit to inherit scene defaults.

enabled.description: Cron only fires when true. Use false to pause without deleting the template.

checks.description: Per-run success criteria evaluated against the completed run.

tasks.description: Scenebench-shaped runnable scenarios. Each task is an initialState + prompt + check rubric, executed against an ephemeral scene fork by the task runner.

runAs.description: Role slug to scope the run's layer permissions. References a `roles[]` slug or built-in (owner|editor|viewer). Defaults to owner-attribution.

outputs.description: (v1 stub — reserved) Named projections from scene state.

principal.description: Canonical principal in '<provider>:<identity>' form. Examples: 'github:mirkokiefer', 'github:315252' (numeric id, rename-proof), 'email:foo@bar.com', 'google:<sub>', 'apple:<sub>'. The provider prefix is the auth-provider slug; identity is unique within that provider. Use this form for new blueprints — it scales to any auth provider including asset-provider-auth (e.g. 'slack:U07ABC'). `email` and `device` below remain as legacy aliases.

email.description: Legacy alias for principal 'email:<addr>'. Resolves only against email-auth users.

device.description: Device slot slug. Provisioning binds physical devices to slots out-of-band (e.g. eFuse pubkey registration).

role.description: Built-in (owner|editor|viewer) or a custom role slug declared in `roles[]`. Anonymous public access is declared on the role itself via `roles[i].public: true`, not on a member entry.

scenes.description: Child scenes nested under this one. parentId is derived from context unless explicit on the child.

check.description: autocheck expression. Returns { pass, gap, why } on evaluation.
